bullets.text: U.S. payrolls fell 92,000 in February; unemployment rate at 4.4%, signaling labor-market cooling and higher recession-risk chatter.
Fed officials split: some see weaker jobs as strengthening the case for rate cuts, while others say the report complicates near-term decisions.
Trump demands Iran’s “unconditional surrender,” escalating stated U.S. war aims and raising risks of a broader, longer conflict.
Iran war is disrupting oil flows and shipping; Maersk suspended key services, highlighting supply-chain and trade risks tied to Gulf transit routes.
Oil surged to $90+ and posted a record weekly gain (~35%); gasoline prices jumped, quickly feeding into consumer inflation expectations.
Oil shock drove Treasurys’ worst weekly rout in months, underscoring the inflation-versus-growth tradeoff markets are repricing.
Defense sector momentum: Trump says defense CEOs will sharply boost “Exquisite Class” output; defense-tech stocks and Palantir rallied on wartime demand.
S&P 500 index changes add four companies, including AI/data-center exposure—reinforcing passive-flow support for infrastructure tied to the AI buildout.
Tariff legal battles intensify: Customs and Border Protection says it can’t comply with a refund order; Nintendo also sues the U.S. for tariff refunds.
FDA leadership and policy uncertainty: vaccine chief to step down; recent FDA reversals are spooking biotech investors about regulatory unpredictability.
Anthropic’s Claude turbulence: cloud giants say it remains available outside defense work; Claude helped find 22 Firefox vulnerabilities, boosting security credibility.
NASA’s DART impact measurably changed an asteroid’s orbit around the sun, strengthening the scientific basis for planetary-defense deflection strategies.
New research argues global warming has accelerated significantly, implying faster-moving climate risks for policy, insurance, infrastructure, and energy transitions.
